title: US intensifies attacks on Iran as Houthis block Red Sea, oil surges
summary: US forces launch attacks on Iran for tenth consecutive day, while Houthi rebels declare a blockade in the Red Sea that pushes oil prices to one-month highs and roils global markets.

title: Takaichi government approves economic policy framework and pushes forward with 'vice capital'
summary: Prime Minister Takaichi's administration pushes law to create a 'vice capital' and details its economic agenda amid extended parliamentary debate.

title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian dairy products
summary: Trump administration announces additional 50% tariff on Canadian products, including dairy, accusing Ottawa of discriminating against US goods, with effect expected in August.

title: Spain celebrates World Cup title while FIFA investigates violent incidents
summary: The Spanish team parades in Madrid celebrating its world championship, amid investigations into violent acts in the final and criticism of the tournament's politicization.

title: Tokyo stock market rises but yen weakens against strong dollar
summary: The Nikkei index posts gains driven by bargain hunting and semiconductor rises, while the yen falls to 162 per dollar, its lowest level in decades.

title: Extreme heatwave in Japan with temperatures above 40 degrees
summary: Over 250 observation points record extreme heat days (over 35°C), with heatstroke warnings in large parts of the country, including temperatures up to 40°C in several regions.

title: Ukraine attacks Russian logistics in Moscow with over 400 drones
summary: In retaliation for Russian bombings, Ukraine launches a massive wave of drones against logistics facilities near Moscow, while North Korea reaffirms its support for Russia.

title: Wall Street falls on fears of war escalation in Middle East
summary: The Dow Jones suffers a decline of more than 300 points dragged by fears of an open conflict between the US and Iran, affecting the financial and consumer sectors.

title: Prince Akishino to officially visit Paraguay in August
summary: Prince and Princess Akishino will make an official visit to Paraguay from August 18 to 26 to commemorate 90 years of Japanese immigration to the South American country.

title: Four arrested for attempted theft of 195 million yen at Haneda Airport
summary: Police arrest four suspects for stealing 195 million yen in cash at Haneda Airport, allegedly using irritant spray against victims.

title: Chinese and Russian warships conduct live-fire exercises in Japanese EEZ
summary: Japan's Navy monitors Chinese and Russian vessels conducting live-fire drills inside Japan's Exclusive Economic Zone, near Okinotorishima Island.

title: New UK Prime Minister Burnham forms cabinet with shift toward 'big government'
summary: Keir Burnham's Labour government appoints former Defense Secretary as Finance Minister and prioritizes decentralization, in a shift from Thatcher's legacy.

title: US Justice Department demands phone records of New York Times journalist
summary: The US government asks the phone company for call records of a New York Times reporter and his family, drawing criticism over press freedom.

title: Daycare center convicted for death of baby neglected while sleeping face down
summary: A court sentences a daycare center for negligence after a baby's death by suffocation, in a case that highlights lack of awareness about childcare.

title: EU imposes record fine of 102 billion yen on AliExpress
summary: The European Union fines Chinese e-commerce giant AliExpress a historic penalty for failing to take sufficient measures against the sale of illegal products.

title: Japan introduces numerical criteria to define dangerous driving
summary: From July 21, a new law sets objective thresholds to define reckless driving, although some violations may be penalized even below those limits.

title: Toyota president seeks to maintain domestic production in face of Trump tariffs
summary: Toyota president Akio Toyoda says the company prioritizes maintaining its production and supply chain in Japan, resisting the impact of US tariffs.
